remarks: Built in 1918, this timeless Dutch Colonial in Stamford's Glenbrook neighborhood blends New England character with thoughtful updates, set on a level 0.40-acre lot with genuine curb appeal. Inside, nearly 2,000 square feet of sunlit living space includes four bedrooms, two and a half bathrooms, hardwood floors throughout, and a living room anchored by a gas fireplace. The home has been meticulously maintained, balancing its historic character with today's conveniences. The kitchen is the heart of the house, with marble countertops, a breakfast nook, and an adjoining mudroom that opens directly to the backyard, ideal for everyday life and entertaining alike. Upstairs, the renovated primary suite includes a custom closet system and a spa-inspired bath. Recent improvements include roof, central air conditioning, and a hydro-air heating system that replaced the original steam radiators. Outside, a deck with pergola overlooks a fully fenced, tree-lined yard with established perennial gardens and a detached garage, a private retreat built for summer gatherings, morning coffee, and everyday living. Minutes from Cove Island Park, Metro-North, and downtown Stamford, this is a home that offers timeless character alongside real, everyday convenience.
